item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded:
Noting that a recent series of tags were removed, as there were a few tags missing.

Readers, if you're not going to add all the tags for your article, it's best to just leave the page untagged and wait for an experienced tagger to take care of it. We have a team of people on the site who tag articles, and they search for pages that have no tags. Adding only a few tags prevents the team from finding the article, so if you're unfamiliar with the tags, it's totally fine to wait for them to take care of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That twist at the end really won me over. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The argument can be made for keter, so maybe I'll tinker with the description to make this a clear safe class object. I'll isolate them to farms in the northeast atlantic or something. The point of my execution was to basically write a Series I magic animal as the cover up document, and then give it that modern twist in the level 4 version.

To answer how we found out about them, since they don't die and are all 100% identical to one another (and black), farmers would definitely notice that. Easterns are generally gray or cream colored, so a bunch of black ones would likely get tossed, and eventually the FDA would probably get called if a ton of black oysters kept showing up.

EDIT: Made it specify that the oysters can only reproduce with other anomalous oysters. Given the life cycle of oysters, this means they are exceedingly rare. Also explains why the bloom had to happen on a farm. Hope that alleviates the issue.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Shouldn't this be Keter, since there could be any number in public hands?
Also, how did the Foundation find out about it before it exhibited the knowledge effect? Surely the pink skin and dark shell alone wouldn't alert them.

Still pretty good, I'm just a pessimist. +1

Edit: I'm a loser and didn't click the collapsible. Still advocating for the Keter though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Quality stuff, and a nice clean twist.

👌🏽

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Lol I know that. You did upboat after all! Was more of just a general statement on the name gag. It seems to have gone through periods of taboo and not taboo, which is interesting

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: oh i didn't mean to be mean, i thought it was fuckin hilarious

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeahhh, I know the name gag may turn a few people off here and here, but I think it works better as a Foundation intranet handle than like a "the skip is coming for you next, reader" gag. Even if it isn't super believable, its not horribly immersion breaking imo

But hey, personal preference and all that jazz

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is pretty interesting and wild

My favorite part is where it acts like my wikidot name is an actual real code name that a foundation researcher would use

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While the O5 no contact thing is generally true, there are a few commonly accepted exceptions (though there is no canon, so feel free to disagree). Notably, Thaumiels that aid the Foundation in accomplishing their mission (like SCP-2000) and SCPs that directly benefit the person interacting with them in some way (like the Fountain of Youth).

I have a very specific version of the O5 Council in my headcanon, and big part of that involves disinformation within the Foundation (hence fake documents, idea theft, and using skips when they say they won't). These guys have a lot of power and do not want to lose it. To them, anybody keeping them in check is an enemy, even if that means people like the Ethics Committee.

Hope this clarified my personal philosophies a little bit! Also, welcome to the site!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yea i just read the additional "…but in secret, sometimes they do." in the collapsible so im the dumbass here

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Right, but who enforces that rule? The ethics committee? Maybe the 05's are looking for a little freedom…

Hmmm, and I just realized SCP-006 also breaks that rule. I guess it must not be a hard and fast one. I think that thematically, it's okay for the 05's to interact with the SCP here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So first of all this is really good! Especially the gimmick of only getting most of the information when you put in your credentials is really neat (also this account is relatively new so idk if any other page did it but my actual username showing up was a cool surprise).

You even managed to make the entire scip before the credentials entirely believable. The only time I thought something more was going on was at the test log because there were only the two of them.

That said, according to the Personnel and Character Dossier the 05's "…do not and cannot come into contact with any anomalous entities…" so seeing that later on took me a little out of it

But yea great job on your second scip! I'll be looking forward to more

item.title: The World Is Your Oyster
item.content:encoded:
Came the last night of sadness
And it was clear she couldn't go on
The door was open and the wind appeared
The candles blew and then disappeared
The curtains flew and then he appeared
Saying don't be afraid

Well, here's my second SCP! This ended up being far more ambitious than I'd originally intended, but I'm very proud of how it turned out.

Special thanks to:

  • Modern_Erasmus for giving me the push I needed to make these babies Thaumiel way back in I&B.
  • MathBrush, Zyn, and Stallmantic for their amazing line-by-line critiques, as well as dealing with my neurotic ass in the first place. εїз εїз εїз Y'all are dope as hell.
  • Rounderhouse for doing their absolute best to parse through my mad ravings on a panicked I&B rehash thread.
+ Image Attribution
- Image Attribution

"Eastern Oyster (Crassostrea virginica) Top" by Andrew Cannizzaro. Attribution 2.0 Generic (CC BY 2.0). This image has not been altered in any way.
